Acorn Hill Breakdown: Same key (D), melody, AABB form and chords. New is a clean Finale 2020 re-engraving of the same setting - clearer noteheads and beaming, no musical change.
Clearwater Stomp: Same key (G), melody and chords (G/D7/A7/B7/E7). New makes the repeat explicit - adds '1st ending / 2nd ending' labels with a repeat barline at the line-4 cadence - and adds the attribution 'from Gil Kieseker'. Otherwise the same setting.
Cricket On a Hearth: Same arrangement (Floyd Engstrom), key (D), melody, chords, the 'Alternate measures 11 & 12' option and the 'rhythm rest' note. Essentially identical content; new is just a cleaner, more compact scan.
Far From Home: Same key (G) and largely the same melody, but a DIFFERENT chord setting - the B part is Em-D-C-G in the new vs Em-Bm-Em-G in the current, and the A-part chords are reordered. New also drops the 'Arr. Cathie Whitesides' attribution. Worth a close look before swapping.
Fisher's Hornpipe: Same key (D), tune and AABB form with 1st/2nd endings. New is a different transcriber's copy ('Stuart Williams version'): chords written as A7 (vs plain A) and the B part adds E7-A7-G; a couple of triplets marked. Melody essentially the same.
Gypsy Waltz: Same key (Gm/Bb), 3/4, melody, chords, triplets and the 'Da Capo / Final ending' scheme. New is a slightly cleaner scan that adds one chord label (A7 at the start of line 2). Otherwise identical.
Laura Susan: Same key (F), 2/4, melody, chords (F/C7/Bb) and form (Dudley Briggs). New is a clean re-render and adds one C7 label at the end of line 3. No musical change.
Lost Indian: Same key (D), melody, a)/b1)/b2) structure, chords (D/Bm/G/A7) and 1st/2nd endings. New is a clean Finale re-engraving; it drops the handwritten 'Evergreen Fiddler Tunebook Vol.3' note and a couple of stray chord labels. No musical change.
MacArthur Road: Same key (E), melody, chords (E/f#m/A/B) and form with 1st/2nd endings (David Richardson). New is a clean Finale 2020 re-engraving and adds the attribution 'as played by Alasdair Fraser'. No musical change.
Me and My Fiddle: Same key (G), melody and chords (Benny Martin). The ending instruction differs: current uses a Coda with 'D.C. al Coda'; new drops the Coda and instead marks 'End with 1A'. Melody and chords otherwise the same.
Miss McCloud's Reel: Same tune, key (G) and chords (G/C/D7), plus the same 'also known as Uncle Joe / Hop High Ladies' note. The big difference is legibility: current is a dense, nearly-illegible old engraving; new is a clean modern Finale transcription of the same setting - a major readability upgrade.
Monkey in the Dogcart: Same key (C), melody, chords and AABB form with 1st/2nd endings. New is a clean, more compact Finale re-engraving; final cadence chord shown as G (vs G7) and an extra G label added near the end. No real musical change.
Moonlight: Same key (D), melody, chords (D/G/A) and 6-line form with repeats (Bruce Green, fiddle). New is a clean Finale re-engraving - no musical change.
Morrison's Jig: Same tune, key (E dorian, 6/8), chords (Em/D/G/Bm) and (A)/(B) structure ending on Em. New is the same content at much better contrast/legibility than the faded current scan.
None of Your Business: Same key (C, cut time), melody, chords (C/G/Am/D) and 1st/2nd endings (Garry Harrison). Essentially identical content; the new scan is slightly skewed. No musical change.
Red Apple Rag: Same tune and key (G). The new (Arthur Smith) ADDS chord symbols (G/C/D7) that the current scan has none of, makes the AABB repeat structure explicit, and is a clean 4/4 engraving (current was an un-chorded cut-time scan marked 'Quick').
Red River Cart Polka: Same key (G), 2/4, melody, chords (G/Am/D/C/D7), tr ornaments and a)/b) structure (Andy De Jarlis). New is essentially the same scan - no musical change.
Road House Ramble: Same key (G), melody, chords and bowing (Reg Bouvette / arr. Patti Kusturok). The ending instruction is re-annotated: current's 'To Coda' + coda sign becomes handwritten 'To final ending' / 'Final Ending'. Melody and chords unchanged.
Ross's Reel #4: Same key (F, cut time), melody, chords (C7/F/C) and 1st/2nd endings in both parts. New is the same content, a slightly lower-resolution scan. No musical change.
Salmon Tails Up the Water: Same key (G), 2/4, melody and chords (G/C/D) (Northumbrian Rant). New is the same content, slightly cleaner and smaller. No musical change.
Stone's Rag: Same key (C/A), 4/4, melody, chords (A/C/F/dm/G7/A7/D7/G) and A/B form with 'Final ending' (fiddle). New is the same content; it drops the large bold (A)/(B) section letters. No musical change.
Waverly Two-Step: Same key (D), melody, chords (D/A/G/E), bowing and measure numbering (Glenn Berry, violin). New is the same content at slightly lighter contrast. No musical change.
Woodchopper's Reel: Same key (D, cut time), melody, chords (D/A) and AABB form with triplets (Ned Landry). New ADDS two handwritten 'rhythm break ->' performance annotations in the B part; otherwise identical.
